locations.raw_source_name: The Astrophysical Journal, Vol 954, Iss 2, p 209 (2023)

crossref.abstract:
<jats:title>Abstract</jats:title>
               <jats:p>We describe the ensemble properties of the 1.9 < <jats:italic>z</jats:italic> < 3.5 Lyman alpha emitters (LAEs) found in the HETDEX survey’s first public data release, HETDEX Public Source Catalog 1. Stacking the low-resolution (<jats:italic>R</jats:italic> ∼ 800) spectra greatly increases the signal-to-noise ratio (S/N), revealing spectral features otherwise hidden by noise, and we show that the stacked spectrum is representative of an average member of the set. The flux-limited, Ly<jats:italic>α</jats:italic> S/N restricted stack of 50,000 HETDEX LAEs shows the ensemble biweight <jats:italic>average</jats:italic>
                  <jats:italic>z</jats:italic> ∼ 2.6 LAE to be a blue (UV continuum slope ∼ −2.4 and <jats:italic>E(B – V)</jats:italic> < 0.1), moderately bright (<jats:italic>M</jats:italic>
                  <jats:sub>UV</jats:sub> ∼ −19.7) star-forming galaxy with strong Ly<jats:italic>α</jats:italic> emission (log <jats:italic>L</jats:italic>
                  <jats:sub>Ly<jats:italic>α</jats:italic>
                  </jats:sub> ∼ 42.8 and <jats:italic>W</jats:italic>
                  <jats:sub>
                     <jats:italic>λ</jats:italic>
                  </jats:sub>(Ly<jats:italic>α</jats:italic>) ∼ 114 Å), and potentially significant leakage of ionizing radiation. The rest-frame UV light is dominated by a young, metal-poor stellar population with an average age of 5–15 Myr and metallicity of 0.2–0.3 <jats:italic>Z</jats:italic>
                  <jats:sub>⊙</jats:sub>.</jats:p>
title:
HETDEX Public Source Catalog 1—Stacking 50,000 Lyman Alpha Emitters
                  <sup>∗</sup>
